Curriculum Vitae · Food Innovation · Digital Innovation · Artificial Intelligence

María Morillo Cabello

Director of Digital Innovation · IDEA FOOD CENTER

PhD in the field of consumer protection and functional foods, with a long-standing track record in agri-food R&D, healthy bakery products, nutrition and health claims, food labelling, sustainability and digital innovation. Her profile combines industrial food formulation, regulatory-scientific assessment and the strategic use of artificial intelligence to support professional productivity, technical documentation and innovation processes.

AI applied to business and R&DHealthy bakery formulationSustainable food systemsNutrition & health claimsEU and national R&D projectsFood labelling and regulation
Organisation: IDEA FOOD CENTER · www.ideafoodcenter.es
Email: maria.morillo@ideafoodcenter.es
Current position since: November 2025
Location: Spain

Core expertise

  • Development of R&D projects for agri-food companies.
  • Design and manufacture of new food products, with strong expertise in bakery and cereal-based products.
  • Functional foods, nutritional improvement and substantiation of food-related claims.
  • Food labelling, consumer information and regulatory-scientific interpretation.
  • Sustainability, healthier food systems and innovation transfer.
  • Artificial intelligence applied to professional workflows, documentation and innovation strategy.

Academic background

1997BSc in ChemistryUniversity of Valladolid.
2000Research ProficiencyDepartment of Chemical Engineering, University of Valladolid.
2009–2013Doctoral Thesis“The protection of the European consumer in the field of functional foods”. Faculty of Education and Social Work, University of Valladolid. Passed with Cum Laude distinction.

AI training

2024 · CertifiedPrompt Engineering Strategies for ChatGPTFormal certification issued by OTECH Capacitación and certified by SCI Certificaciones S.A.C. Completed online with a grade of 6.4/7.
2022–Present · 3 yearsAI Academy — Javier MitjanaContinuous training in practical artificial intelligence applied to business and digital productivity, including generative AI, workflow automation and AI tool integration.
2024–PresentAI Master Academy — Ángel AparicioAdvanced programme focused on the strategic and operational deployment of AI in professional environments, including LLM applications, AI agents, content creation and research automation.

Professional networks

  • Member of the “Food and Health” Working Group, Food for Life Platform.
  • Member of the “Food and Consumer” Working Group, Food for Life Platform.
  • Reviewer for the journal Nutrición Hospitalaria.

Strategic professional profile

Artificial intelligence

Current professional focus on digital innovation and applied AI, with continuous training in generative AI, prompt engineering, workflow automation, AI agents and the integration of AI tools into professional and sector-specific environments.

Sustainability

Experience in projects and training related to sustainable food systems, ecological ingredients, sustainable diets, environmental variables in project development and new food products aligned with healthier and more sustainable consumption patterns.

Healthy bakery products

Extensive R&D experience in biscuits, cookies, cereal products and baked snacks, including sugar and fat reduction, prebiotic enrichment, probiotic cereal products, acrylamide reduction and products aimed at specific nutritional needs.

Regulatory-scientific assessment

Specialisation in nutrition and health claims, consumer protection in functional foods, labelling, evidence assessment and communication of complex technical and scientific information for professional audiences.

Selected R&D projects in bakery, nutrition, sustainability and health

Substitution of sodium metabisulphite in gluten conditioning for biscuit manufacturing
CDTI / Ministry of Science and Technology · 1998–1999

Feasibility study on replacing sodium metabisulphite with natural enzymes, animal and plant proteases or amino acids.

Design and manufacture of bakery products and snacks with beneficial health properties
CDTI · 2000–2002

Development of baked snacks, sandwich biscuits with low cariogenic activity and symbiotic properties, and chocolate bakery products with reduced sugar and fat content.

Organic biscuit product range
CDTI and ADE · 2002–2003

Design of high-quality biscuits with at least 95% certified organic ingredients of agricultural origin.

GALFUN — Functional biscuit with beneficial health effects
CDTI IBEROEKA and Ministry of Industry · 2004–2006

Development of a functional biscuit and the scientific evidence required to support claims under the emerging European regulatory framework.

COOKIDS — Healthier biscuits for childhood obesity prevention
CDTI · 2008–2009

Development of healthier children’s biscuits designed to fit within a balanced diet.

HIGEA — Prevention of chronic food-related diseases
CDTI CENIT · 2007–2010

Industrial research on preventive food-based strategies to reduce risk factors associated with obesity, diabetes and cardiovascular diseases.

SARA — Ingredients and foods for specific nutritional needs
CDTI Technology Fund · 2011–2013

Development of ingredients with scientifically demonstrated biological activity for specific nutritional needs.

Scientific substantiation guide for health claims
CDTI INNPRONTA · 2011–2014

Scientific validation of the efficacy of a functional biscuit in relation to immune function, cognitive function and metabolic syndrome.

PRIMICIA — Personalised nutrition for high-efficacy foods
CDTI CIEN · 2014–2017

Research into personalised nutritional strategies and food products aimed at controlling chronic inflammation through diet adapted to genetic profiles.

METASIN — Foods to reduce biomarkers of metabolic syndrome
CDTI CIEN · 2015–2018

Development of active ingredients, foods and multifunctional food supplements targeting metabolic syndrome risk factors.

NUTRIPRECISIÓN — Precision nutrition for pre-senior and senior groups
CDTI CIEN · 2016–2019

Design of bakery products aimed at improving quality of life in pre-senior and senior populations.

TOLERA — Food allergies and intolerances
CDTI CIEN · 2018–2021

Research into ingredients, technologies and safer food solutions for populations with food allergies and intolerances.

COLOR — Acrylamide reduction in processed foods
ERDF Interconecta-CDTI · 2018–2021

Reduction of acrylamide formation in biscuits and fried potatoes, and development of an indirect qualitative detection method.

PROBIOMIC — Cereal products with probiotics for infant nutrition
CDTI Cervera Technology Centres · 2018–2021

Development of cereal-based probiotic products with improved nutritional profile and no refrigeration requirement.

NOVACARNI — Healthy and sustainable cured meat product
ICE Castilla y León · 2022–2024

Development of a cured meat product with vegetables and/or legumes to support healthier and more sustainable consumer choices.

SALSEJO — Vegan sauces from Verdejo wine and vinegar
ICE · 2024–2025

Sustainable vineyard management and formulation of vegan sauces and creams based on Verdejo wine and vinegar from Rueda Designation of Origin.

VITABEET — Enrichment of processed beetroot for 5th range products
ICE · 2024–2026

Study of vitamin C and iron enrichment strategies in cooked processed beetroot, addressing stability after high-temperature sterilisation and throughout shelf life.

Publications

  • Morillo Cabello, M. et al. “Active and intelligent packaging: a promising vision for the future”. Eurocarne, 2005.
  • López, R.; Torres, T.; Blanco, B.; Morillo, M.; Antolín, G. “Food packaging and preservation: latest trends”. Alimentación, Equipos y Tecnología, 2004.
  • Morillo, M. et al. “Nutrition based on products from organic farming”. AXIS, 2003.
  • Morillo, M. et al. “Organic nutrition and lifestyle. False organic claims: consumer confusion”. AXIS, 2003.
  • De Luis, D.A. et al. “Randomised clinical trial with an inulin-enriched biscuit on the cardiovascular risk pattern of obese patients”. Nutrición Hospitalaria, 2010;25(1):53–59.
  • De Luis, D.A. et al. “Double blind randomized clinical trial controlled by placebo with an alpha-linolenic acid and prebiotic enriched cookie on cardiovascular risk factor in obese patients”. Nutrición Hospitalaria, 2011;26(4):827–833.
  • Morillo Cabello, M. “Implications of nutrition labelling and health claims in the healthcare field”. Nutrición Clínica en Medicina, 2019;XIII(3):153–173.
  • Morillo Cabello, M. “What is a sustainable diet in the 21st century?”. Nutrición Clínica en Medicina, 2022;XVI(2):81–93.

Teaching, courses and invited talks

Extensive experience as speaker, lecturer and tutor in food technology, nutrition, food labelling, health claims, sustainable diets, Nutri-Score/Eco-Score, clinical nutrition and digital transformation in the agri-food sector. This includes university teaching at the University of Valladolid and UNIR, continuing education courses, professional seminars and sectoral events.

  • Lecturer in the Master’s Degree in Quality, Development and Innovation of Foods, University of Valladolid.
  • Lecturer in the Master’s Degree in Digital Transformation and Innovation in the Agri-food Sector, UNIR, academic years 2022–2023 and 2023–2024.
  • Online tutor for ESI Learning in food quality systems, health, nutrition and dietetics, food safety management systems and waste management.
  • Invited talks on nutrition and health claims, food labelling, sustainable diets and innovation in healthy and sustainable food systems.
